title: Reserve Bank of India v. Jayantilal N. Mistry
legal_principles: RBI's fiduciary duty to banks does not override RTI obligations
summary: RBI resisted RTI disclosure of bank inspection reports citing Section 8(1)(e) of RTI Act (fiduciary relationship). Supreme Court held that RBI does not stand in a fiduciary relationship with banks — it is a regulator, not an advisor. Therefore, inspection reports and related information must be disclosed under RTI Act, subject to specific exemptions.
key_holdings: RBI is not in a fiduciary relationship with banks — it is a regulator
Section 8(1)(e) exemption (fiduciary relationship) does not protect RBI from RTI disclosures
Inspector General/RBI reports can be disclosed unless specifically exempted
Public interest in banking transparency outweighs confidentiality claims
RTI Act applies fully to RBI's regulatory activities
why_important: Significant for banking transparency and RTI rights of citizens to access banking regulatory information.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Relevant to banking compliance, RBI inspection obligations, and financial transparency requirements.
